title: Real-Time Automated Defect Detection Pipeline
description: Developed a real-time computer vision system for manufacturing quality control. Built an end-to-end pipeline from data augmentation to live webcam inference, emphasizing computational profiling for future edge deployment.
longDescription: This project establishes a foundational architecture for industrial visual inspection. Utilizing a public dataset of manufacturing anomalies (such as PCB soldering defects or metal casting flaws), a custom Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and YOLO-based models were trained using PyTorch. The system performs live inference via OpenCV. To ensure readiness for constrained embedded hardware, the project strictly profiles engineering metrics: tracking model footprint in megabytes, calculating floating-point operations per second (FLOPs), and benchmarking inference latency across CPU and GPU execution.

title: ROS2 Autonomous Navigation & SLAM System
description: Engineered an autonomous mobile robot simulation in ROS2 and Gazebo. Implemented full SLAM mapping, sensor simulation, and dynamic path planning.
longDescription: This project demonstrates high-level robotic system architecture and autonomous navigation within a simulated environment. A differential drive robot was modeled using URDF and deployed in Gazebo, complete with simulated LiDAR and odometry sensors. Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) was executed via slam_toolbox to generate accurate 2D occupancy grids. The ROS2 Nav2 stack was configured to handle global path planning (A*/Dijkstra) and local obstacle avoidance. The architecture focuses on node separation, topic communication, and system profiling using rqt_graph.

title: High-Speed Line Follower Robot
description: Designed and programmed a fast autonomous line follower. Implemented efficient control logic for high-speed path tracking.
achievements: 🥇 1st place + 2 Gold Medals at the Madagascar Cup of Robotics

title: Maze Solver Robot
description: Built an autonomous robot capable of navigating and solving maze paths. Developed navigation algorithms and sensor-based decision logic for real-time movement.
longDescription: The Maze Solver Robot is an autonomous vehicle designed to find the shortest path from a starting point to the center of a maze. It relies on a combination of ultrasonic and infrared sensors for wall detection and distance measurement. The internal logic implements popular algorithms like Wall Follower or Flood Fill to guarantee a solution.

title: Robotics Competition Robots
description: Designed and built two fully functional, competition-ready robots as part of Madagascar’s selected national team for Robotex International.
longDescription: Chosen to represent Madagascar globally, this project involved the end-to-end conceptualization, 3D modeling, electronics integration, and software development of two distinct competition robots. It required rigorous system testing to ensure reliability and compliance with international competition rules.

title: Automatic Plant Watering System
description: Designed an automated irrigation system based on soil moisture sensing, implementing control logic for efficient water usage.
achievements: 🥉 3rd place at the MADA ROBOT CONTEST
longDescription: Addressing the need for sustainable agriculture, this automated plant watering system monitors soil moisture levels in real-time. When humidity dips below a defined threshold, the microcontroller activates a localized water pump, ensuring optimal hydration without water waste.

title: Smart Greenhouse Control System
description: Developed an ESP32-based smart greenhouse controller utilizing a fuzzy logic control system for adaptive environmental regulation (Temperature, Soil moisture, Air humidity, Lighting). Features multi-sensor data acquisition, real-time actuator control, and a web-based dashboard.
longDescription: A comprehensive IoT solution for agricultural environment control. Powered by an ESP32, the system reads data from multiple specialized sensors (Temperature, Soil moisture, Air humidity, and lighting levels). Instead of simple thresholds, it utilizes a Fuzzy Logic control system to make nuanced decisions, activating fans, heaters, or pumps proportionately to maintain ideal growing conditions. Data is logged and accessible via a responsive web dashboard.
